reasoning: The base value on 2025-10-26 is highly likely to be the October 2025 value (0.973), which is very low compared to the historical 1-year average of ~1.49. The series shows significant volatility with clear mean-reversion characteristics (both previous dips below 1 resulted in strong upward bounds in following months). While there is a slight downward trend over the past year reflecting macroeconomic conditions, a short-term bounce-back above the 0.97 threshold is highly probable, so the probability for the first resolution date is set above 0.6. As the forecast horizon increases, long-term downward drifts in interest rates may start to compound or the true long-term mean may have shifted downward, increasing model uncertainty. Therefore, in accordance with the instruction that uncertainty expands (bringing estimates closer to 0.5 for more distant horizons), the second probability is moderated toward 0.5.
